summary: Completed comprehensive backend testing of Business Radar AI architectural refactor (Alembic, rate limiting, idempotency, scheduler, health monitor, reports, RBAC). 37/44 tests passed (84.1%). Core architectural features working correctly. Found 7 failing tests related to report PDF generation and some endpoint 500 errors.

critical_bugs.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error when generating reports
critical_bugs.impact: Blocks report generation for Admin and Analyst roles
critical_bugs.details: Tested with valid payload {report_date: '2026-06-14', filters: {}}. Error is caught by global exception handler, actual error not visible in logs. Likely issue with PDF generation or storage service.

critical_bugs.issue: Returns report_json but report_pdf is None/empty
critical_bugs.impact: PDF reports not being generated or stored
critical_bugs.details: Report JSON is generated correctly but PDF URI is missing. Storage provider is 'local' but PDF not being persisted.

critical_bugs.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error for Subscriber role
critical_bugs.details: Works for Admin/Analyst but fails for Subscriber. Possible issue with query complexity or schema serialization for Subscriber role.

critical_bugs.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error for Subscriber role
critical_bugs.details: Works for Admin/Analyst but fails for Subscriber. Similar to businesses endpoint issue.

passed_tests: Alembic migration: All 15 tables exist, alembic_version at head '0001_initial_schema'
Alembic: create_all() not used in production code (only in poc_core.py and migration file)
Alembic: Database accessible via /api/healthz
Rate Limit: Burst limit triggered (5 calls/10s) with 429 response
Rate Limit: 429 includes Retry-After header
Rate Limit: Successful login after burst window clears counter
Idempotency: First discovery run succeeds
Idempotency: Second request with same key returns cached result (no new inserts)
Idempotency: Same key with different payload returns 409 Conflict
Scheduler: PATCH schedule_cron succeeds and updates DB
Scheduler: Invalid cron accepted but scheduler skips it (logs warning)
Health: GET /api/discovery/health returns correct structure (totals, queue, sources)
Health: Source-level health has rolling_24h stats with all expected keys
Health: Alert levels are green/amber/red
Health: Run count increases after discovery run
Health: last_success_at populated after successful run
RBAC: Admin can PATCH /api/discovery/sources/{id}
RBAC: Admin can POST /api/discovery/queue/process
RBAC: Analyst can POST /api/discovery/run
RBAC: Analyst can POST /api/discovery/sources/{id}/run
RBAC: Analyst CANNOT PATCH /api/discovery/sources (403 correct)
RBAC: Subscriber can GET /api/reports
RBAC: Subscriber CANNOT POST /api/discovery/run (403 correct)
RBAC: Subscriber CANNOT PATCH /api/discovery/sources (403 correct)
RBAC: Subscriber CANNOT POST /api/reports/generate (403 correct)
RBAC: Unauthenticated GET /api/businesses returns 401
RBAC: Public endpoints /api/ and /api/healthz accessible without auth
Alembic Migration State: 15 tables exist, version at head
Rate Limiting: Burst and sustained limits working with 429 + Retry-After
Idempotency: Cached responses and 409 conflict detection working
Scheduler: Dynamic schedule registration and updates working
Health Monitoring: Rolling 24h stats with alert levels working
RBAC: Most role-based permissions working correctly

priority_1_critical: Fix POST /api/reports/generate 500 error - investigate PDF generation and storage service
Fix GET /api/reports/today to properly generate and store PDF (report_pdf is None)
Debug why report PDF generation is failing - check reportlab library and storage_service.py
priority_2_high: Fix GET /api/businesses 500 error for Subscriber role - works for Admin/Analyst
Fix GET /api/dashboard 500 error for Subscriber role - works for Admin/Analyst
Add proper error logging to see actual exceptions (currently caught by global handler)
verification_needed: Verify Alembic migration logs show 'Alembic: upgrade head completed' on startup (confirmed in backend.err.log)
Verify scheduler logs show 'registered discovery schedule: synthetic @ 0 6 * * * IST' (confirmed)
Test report generation end-to-end after fixing PDF issues
